channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cyberattack compromises Australian school student data
item.description: A significant cybersecurity breach has occurred, with a criminal group claiming responsibility for accessing the personal data of Australian school students through a hack on Instructure, an online le...

item.title: NITI Aayog Vice Chairman’s Phone Hacked, Fake Money Requests Circulate on WhatsApp
item.description: On Thursday evening, Ashok Kumar Lahiri, the Vice Chairman of NITI Aayog, reported that his personal phone number was hacked. According to a Facebook post by Lahiri, the hacker used his number to send...

item.title: Sukanya’s Journey Back to Her Ancestral Home in Meerut
item.description: The excerpt from Parul Sharma’s novel 'The Missing Piece' narrates the story of Shiladitya, a man living in London, and the arrival of his daughter, Sukanya, on a freezing winter night. Shiladitya fon...

item.title: Subway Service Restored on Part of Line 2 Following Temporary Closure
item.description: Toronto's subway Line 2 resumed regular service after experiencing a three-hour disruption earlier today. The temporary closure was prompted by an incident involving a motorcyclist who was seriously i...

item.title: Investigation explores possible link between tattoos and later vision complications
item.description: A W5 investigation examines cases in which individuals who received tattoos earlier in life later developed serious vision problems, raising questions about potential long-term health risks linked to ...

item.title: Abdominal Muscle Contractions May Help Clear Waste from the Brain
item.description: New research led by neuroscientist Patrick Drew at Penn State University suggests that the gentle movement of the brain inside the skull is influenced by abdominal muscle contractions. Using advanced ...

item.title: Ontario Monitors Two Cruise Ship Passengers After Hantavirus Exposure
item.description: Health authorities in Ontario are monitoring two Canadian passengers who were exposed to hantavirus while on a cruise ship. The exposure follows the first reported fatality linked to the virus on the ...

item.title: Usman Yusuf Says Northern Leaders Will Scrutinise Peter Obi’s Record Ahead of 2027 Politics
item.description: Former Executive Secretary of the National Health Insurance Scheme (NHIS), Usman Yusuf, don talk say leaders and people from Northern Nigeria go seriously check Peter Obi’s past record as governor of ...

item.title: Google Chrome Installs a 4GB On-Device AI Model Without Explicit User Permission
item.description: Security researcher Alexander Hanff, also known as 'That Privacy Guy,' has revealed that Google Chrome is automatically downloading a 4GB AI model called Gemini Nano onto users' devices without asking...

item.title: Vange Alinsug motivates NU Lady Bulldogs ahead of Game 2 in UAAP Finals
item.description: In the lead-up to Game 2 of the UAAP Season 88 finals, Vange Alinsug has emerged as a key motivator for the National University (NU) Lady Bulldogs. Following their initial performance in Game 1, Alins...

item.title: Decline in Teen Employment Rates Across Canada
item.description: The employment situation for Canadian teenagers has deteriorated significantly in recent years. Statistics Canada reports that for youth aged 15 to 19, the employment rate has dropped by nearly nine p...

item.title: President Marcos holds talks with Thailand and Cambodia leaders to address border issues
item.description: On May 7, 2026, Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. met with the Prime Ministers of Thailand and Cambodia in a significant diplomatic effort to resolve ongoing border disputes. The discussions f...

item.title: Alex Eala resumes practice ahead of her second Rome match
item.description: Filipino tennis player Alex Eala has returned to the practice court in preparation for her second match at the Italian Open in Rome. After a challenging start in the tournament, Eala focused on refini...

item.title: Les Buszynski recalls growing up in pre-lake Canberra
item.description: Les Buszynski, now an honorary professor at the Australian National University's Strategic and Defence Studies Centre, will share memories of his childhood in Canberra before Lake Burley Griffin was f...

item.title: Justice Mohamed Abdulahi Warsame Joins Supreme Court After Judicial Appointment
item.description: Justice Mohamed Abdulahi Warsame has officially been sworn in as a Judge of Kenya's Supreme Court at State House Nairobi. His appointment, made by President William Ruto following a recommendation fro...

item.title: National Rural Development Indaba Focuses on Infrastructure and Inclusive Growth
item.description: The National Integrated Rural Development Indaba kicked off in Bloemfontein, spotlighting the urgent need to advance rural infrastructure and promote an inclusive economy in South Africa. Held from 7 ...

item.title: Rise of One-Person Companies in China Driven by AI Innovation
item.description: China is witnessing a remarkable growth in one-person companies (OPCs), a new business model gaining traction in the AI era. An OPC is legally registered under the Company Law and enjoys limited liabi...

item.title: Goodluck Jonathan says him go consult before deciding on 2027 presidential run
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan don talk say him go reason well and consult widely before e fit decide to run for Nigeria presidential election wey go hold for 2027. For one public engagement wey e...

item.title: Iran’s Historical Resilience Against Foreign Threats
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s repeated threats against Iran, including warnings of annihilation, illustrate a lack of understanding of Iran’s enduring culture and history. Iran has faced numerous invasi...

item.title: Three Australian women face terror and slavery charges after returning from Syria
item.description: Three Australian women with known connections to Islamic State have been arrested in Sydney and Melbourne after returning from years in a Syrian refugee camp. The women are expected to face charges in...
